event: complaint of one week of general malaise
review of systems was also notable for subjective fevers
came to the United States eight years ago
recently traveled back to Australia in the spring of 2020
denies any alcohol or drug use
admitted to having unprotected sex approximately one month ago
on presentation to the outside hospital
serum lactic acid level was initially 4.9 mmol/L
chest radiography which revealed small bilateral pleural effusions
CT of abdomen and pelvis was notable for the liver consisting of multiple cystic lesions
intrahepatic and extrahepatic bile duct dilatation
evidence of a right sided Bartholin gland cyst
ultrasound of the abdomen performed revealed findings consistent with hepatic abscesses
blood cultures from admission resulted in growth of Fusobacterium necrophorum in both sets
arrangements were made to have her transferred to our hospital
oxygen saturation of 100%, however now on 60 L/min, 65% FiO2 high flow nasal cannula
lung exam revealing scattered crackles with normal respiratory effort on high flow nasal cannula
abdominal exam demonstrating mild right upper quadrant abdominal tenderness
genitourinary exam with erythema and edema of the labia without drainage
no evidence of poor dentition or gingivitis on oral exam
urine gonorrhea and chlamydia PCR tests were negative
CT of the neck showed no evidence of abscess formation
CT of chest showed multiple nodular foci throughout the right lung fields
bilateral pleural effusion, right greater than left
with collapse of adjacent lung tissue
Interval worsening of hepatic abscess burden
antibiotic regimen was transitioned from meropenem to piperacillin-tazobactam
patient’s respiratory status improved with diuresis
transferred to the general medicine ward on nasal cannula
underwent an ultrasound guided thoracentesis of the right lung
removal of 800 mL of maroon colored pleural fluid
analysis of the pleural fluid revealed evidence of exudative fluid
gram stain with no organisms and with 4 + granulocytes
no aerobic or anaerobic bacterial growth
Repeat CT of chest, abdomen and pelvis
evidence of bilateral multifocal pneumonia with pleural effusions
concerns for necrotizing pneumonia or developing pulmonary abscess
subcutaneous right perineal region fluid collection concerning for infected Bartholin gland cyst
cardiothoracic surgery and gynecology were consulted
underwent a video-assisted thoracoscopic surgery (VATS) washout and decortication
pleural rind acid fast staining was negative
biopsy revealed fibrinous and organizing pleuritis with fibrinopurulent exudate
cultures revealed 1 + granulocytes with no organisms
pelvic examination by the gynecology team while sedated
revealed a right labial abscess with swelling
drainage of clear serous fluid followed by thick white caseous material
gram stain revealed no organisms with 3 + granulocytes
bacterial culture grew two colonies of Candida albicans
contrasted CT of her abdomen and pelvis done
nonocclusive thrombus within the right common iliac vein
extending into the internal iliac vein
possible right distal external iliac vein nonocclusive thrombus
neither revealed any evidence of valvular vegetations
diagnosis of atypical Lemierre syndrome of urogenital origin
none of several repeat blood cultures since the initial sets at OSH grew organisms
transitioned to intravenous ceftriaxone and oral metronidazole
